title: JSON Injection via Commit Messages in discord-webhook.sh

test_cases.name: Double Quote Injection
test_cases.description: Verify that commit messages containing double quotes are safely escaped and do not break JSON structure
preconditions: Git repo with test commits available

steps.action: Create git commit with message containing double quotes

steps.input: bash scripts/discord-webhook.sh 1 ok 'Test message with quotes'

steps.action: Verify the generated JSON payload is valid
steps.input: Parse output via json.loads() or jq .

test_cases.expected_result: JSON payload is valid; quote characters are escaped as \" in the JSON string; commit message appears correctly in embeds[0].fields[3].value
acceptance_criteria: Content is preserved exactly as input

test_cases.name: Newline Injection
test_cases.description: Verify that commit messages with newlines (LF/CR) are safely encoded as \n in JSON
preconditions: Ability to pass multi-line strings to bash

steps.input: Capture stdout, parse with json library

test_cases.expected_result: JSON is valid; newlines encoded as \n; embeds.fields[3].value contains escaped newlines; when parsed back, original multi-line text is recovered
acceptance_criteria: Parsed value matches original multi-line input

test_cases.name: Backslash Escaping
test_cases.description: Verify that backslashes in paths/messages are escaped as \\ in JSON
test_cases.expected_result: JSON is valid; backslashes properly escaped; when parsed, string matches input exactly

test_cases.name: Tab Character Handling
test_cases.description: Verify that tab characters (\t) in messages are properly escaped in JSON
test_cases.expected_result: JSON is valid; tabs encoded as \t; parsed content is correct

test_cases.name: Unicode & Emoji Support
test_cases.description: Verify that Unicode characters and emojis are preserved correctly in JSON payloads

steps.action: Create commit with Unicode and emoji
Parse JSON and verify Unicode integrity

test_cases.expected_result: JSON is valid; all Unicode characters preserved; emoji renders correctly when parsed; no encoding corruption
acceptance_criteria: JSON is valid UTF-8 and parses successfully
Unicode characters appear exactly as input

test_cases.name: Mixed Special Characters
test_cases.description: Verify handling of messages with multiple types of special characters simultaneously

steps.action: Create commit with mixed special chars
steps.input: Quote " and newline and tab mixed
Parse JSON and compare to original

test_cases.expected_result: JSON is valid; all special characters are properly escaped; parsed content matches original exactly

test_cases.name: JSON Injection Attempt
test_cases.description: Verify that a commit message containing JSON-like structure cannot break the payload or inject fields

steps.action: Create commit with injected JSON object
Parse JSON and verify structure integrity

test_cases.expected_result: JSON is valid; injected JSON appears as a string value, not as additional fields; payload structure is unchanged; no 'malicious' or 'extra_field' keys at root or embed level
acceptance_criteria: Top-level structure has expected fields only (content, embeds)
Injected payload is escaped and appears as string content

test_cases.name: HTML/Script Injection Attempt
test_cases.description: Verify that HTML/JavaScript payloads in commit messages are safely escaped

steps.action: Verify JSON validity and content treatment

test_cases.expected_result: JSON is valid; HTML/script tags appear as literal string values in JSON, not as markup; no interpretation as HTML
acceptance_criteria: Script tags appear as escaped string content
No XSS vector introduced via JSON

test_cases.name: Empty String Handling
test_cases.description: Verify correct handling of empty commit messages or status values

steps.input: Parse and verify all fields present

test_cases.expected_result: JSON is valid; embeds.description is an empty string; no missing fields; payload structure intact

test_cases.name: Very Long Commit Message
test_cases.description: Verify that very long commit messages (1000+ characters) are handled correctly

steps.action: Parse JSON and measure payload size
steps.input: json.loads() and check length of embeds[0].fields[3].value

test_cases.expected_result: JSON is valid; full message content is preserved; payload size is reasonable; no truncation or data loss

acceptance_criteria.criterion: All variables in JSON payload must be safely escaped
Commit messages with quotes, newlines, and special characters must render safely
acceptance_criteria.verification: Parsed JSON content matches input exactly

acceptance_criteria.criterion: JSON injection attempts must be escaped and not introduce new fields
XSS payloads (HTML/script tags) must not be interpreted
acceptance_criteria.verification: Script tags appear as literal strings in JSON

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Unicode and emoji content must be preserved correctly
acceptance_criteria.verification: Unicode characters appear exactly as input in parsed JSON

acceptance_criteria.criterion: jq must be used for all JSON encoding (no string interpolation)
acceptance_criteria.verification: Code review: no heredoc variable interpolation in JSON building

acceptance_criteria.criterion: All edge cases (empty strings, very long messages) must be handled
acceptance_criteria.verification: JSON remains valid in all cases

regression_tests.name: Standard Heartbeat Flow
regression_tests.description: Verify that normal heartbeat messages still work correctly after JSON injection fix
preconditions: DISCORD_WEBHOOK env var set (or test mode)
regression_tests.test_procedure: bash scripts/discord-webhook.sh 1 ok 'System heartbeat running normally'
regression_tests.expected_result: Script executes successfully; JSON payload is generated; Discord notification is sent (or test reports success)
regression_tests.pass_criteria: Script exits with code 0; JSON is valid

regression_tests.name: Status Variations
regression_tests.description: Verify all status types (ok, warning, critical) generate correct emoji and color codes
regression_tests.expected_result: All three calls succeed; correct emoji used (✅, ⚠️, 🚨); correct color codes (65280 for ok, 16711680 for others)
regression_tests.pass_criteria: All three statuses generate valid JSON with correct emoji in content and color in fields

regression_tests.name: Existing Test Suite Passes
regression_tests.description: Verify that the existing Python test suite (8/8 tests) still passes
regression_tests.expected_result: All 8 tests pass (quotes, newlines, backslashes, tabs, unicode, mixed, json_injection, html_like)
regression_tests.pass_criteria: Exit code 0; 8/8 tests pass message displayed

regression_tests.name: jq Availability Check
regression_tests.description: Verify that the script gracefully handles jq availability
test_procedure: Verify jq is in use (no fallback to vulnerable heredoc)
regression_tests.expected_result: jq 1.6+ is available; script uses jq for payload construction
regression_tests.pass_criteria: jq version returned; script uses jq (visible in code review or strace)

regression_tests.name: Integration with Actual Git Commits
regression_tests.description: Verify that real git commit data is extracted and safely included in Discord payload
preconditions: Git repo at /workspaces/Jit with commit history
test_procedure: Verify embeds[0].fields[2].value contains commit hash link
regression_tests.expected_result: Script extracts real commit data; JSON payload includes valid GitHub commit link; commit message is properly escaped
regression_tests.pass_criteria: JSON is valid; commit hash is non-empty; GitHub URL is correct format

regression_tests.name: Color Code Mapping
regression_tests.description: Verify that status values map correctly to Discord color codes
test_procedure: Test with status='ok' -> color should be 65280 (green)
Test with status='warning' -> color should be 16711680 (red)
Test with status='critical' -> color should be 16711680 (red)
Parse JSON and verify embeds[0].color field
regression_tests.expected_result: Color codes are correct for each status; JSON is valid
regression_tests.pass_criteria: All color codes match expected values; JSON parses successfully

regression_tests.name: Missing DISCORD_WEBHOOK Graceful Handling
regression_tests.description: Verify that missing Discord webhook config is handled gracefully
regression_tests.expected_result: Script completes successfully with exit code 0; message indicates webhook is not configured (skipped)
regression_tests.pass_criteria: Exit code 0; warning message printed; no error

execution_order: Run acceptance criteria tests first (TC-001 to TC-010)
Verify AC-001 through AC-007 are met
Then run regression tests (RT-001 to RT-007)
Finally, run full Python test suite (RT-003)

test_data_generation.method: Base64 encoding to safely pass special characters to bash
test_data_generation.rationale: Avoids shell interpretation of special chars during test execution

success_criteria.all_tests_pass: 10/10 acceptance criteria tests pass + 7/7 regression tests pass
success_criteria.security: No JSON injection vectors identified; all payloads safely escaped
success_criteria.performance: Script execution time < 5 seconds per call; no memory leaks

test_ownership.approval_gate: All tests pass + security review approval

schedule.ci_integration: Run on every commit to scripts/discord-webhook.sh
